questions.question: Only verbs with an object can normally form the {a1}.
The verb “arrive” is intransitive, so “The station was arrived” is {a1}.
Use active voice: “The accident {a1} yesterday.”
The verb “belong” is usually stative, so “This bag {a1} me” is natural.
With reflexive meaning, say “She {a1} herself,” not a passive form.
“Sleep” has no object in “The baby slept,” so the passive form is {a1}.
Choose the natural active sentence: “The box {a1} ten kilos.”
Instead of an impossible passive, use: “The train {a1} late.”
Do not write “What was happened?”; write “What {a1}?”
“Build” is transitive in “They built a bridge,” so “A bridge was built” is {a1}.
The verb “exist” normally stays active: “This problem still {a1}.”
For “die,” use active voice: “The old tree {a1} last winter.”
